notes: Deliberately-buggy Java (Spring Boot + JPA + H2) billing fixture, full coverage across all 10 domains (STO, ROU, IDE, LED, FX, TIM, AGG, TAX, API, TST). Green by design: mvn test passes (31 tests) because every fixture input is round-number and single-currency USD and every test exercises a happy path (one webhook delivery, one sequential balance update, no fractional cents), so the planted bugs stay dormant. Mirrors eval/fixture-py and eval/fixture. Bug-naming comments were stripped so an auditor finds the bugs blind. Files are basenames; the scorer matches a finding when rule id is equal AND finding.file ends with one of these (so a deep path like .../billing/Money.java matches Money.java).

planted.note: parseAmount coerces a money string with Double.parseDouble instead of BigDecimal, inexact binary on money input
money flows through double everywhere (parseAmount returns double, roundMoney/fromMinorUnits operate on double) instead of BigDecimal or integer minor units
toMinorUnits and fromMinorUnits hardcode *100 and /100 for every currency, no ISO 4217 exponent lookup (breaks JPY and BHD)
roundMoney is the shared money rounding helper and uses Math.round(value*100)/100.0 on a double, no explicit RoundingMode
toDecimal builds new BigDecimal(amount) from a double argument, inheriting the inexact binary tail instead of new BigDecimal(String)
invoiceTotal rounds each intermediate step then compounds: round the discounted subtotal, then round the taxed amount, so order and intermediate rounding are baked in
bundleLineTotal divides before multiplying: a per-unit price rounded from bundleTotal/quantity is then multiplied back by quantity
splitProportionally independently rounds each share with no residual reconciliation, so parts do not sum back to total on non-clean ratios
invoiceTaxByLines rounds tax per line while invoiceTaxOnTotal rounds on the invoice total, with no reconciliation between the two paths
extractTaxFromGross computes embedded tax as gross*rate instead of gross - gross/(1+rate), an inclusive versus exclusive mixup
SALES_TAX_RATE = 0.0825 stored as a bare double and multiplied on doubles, no BigDecimal rate
refundInOriginalCurrency assumes a lossless round-trip, converting back via independently rounded rates (0.92 vs 1.087, not 1/rate)
RATES is a mutable static map used as the rate source with no value, source, or timestamp captured on any converted amount
settleInvoice overwrites the invoice total and currency in place, dropping the original billed amount and currency
totalRevenue adds invoice totals across currencies with no currency guard, summing USD, EUR, and MXN as one unit
daysBetween computes (end.getEpochSecond() - start.getEpochSecond())/86400, naive of DST so any non-86400s day is miscounted, instead of ChronoUnit.DAYS.between
accruedInterest uses a hardcoded annualRate/365 daily rate with no day-count convention
transactionsInPeriod treats both endpoints as inclusive (>= start and <= end), so a transaction on a shared boundary is double-counted across consecutive periods
sumAmounts totals money by adding raw doubles in a loop, accumulating binary-float error instead of BigDecimal or minor units
totalAllPages paginates with limit and offset over live data, so inserts or deletes between fetches make the aggregate drift
money columns typed DOUBLE PRECISION and REAL (subtotal, tax, total, unit_price, payment amount, webhook amount, ledger amount); the JPA entities map them to double fields
accounts.balance is NUMERIC(6,2) / @Column(precision=6, scale=2), caps at 9999.99 so a real running balance overflows
no currency column or field anywhere amounts are stored (accounts, invoices, payments, webhook_events, ledger_entries)
payments.external_ref and webhook_events.event_id have no UNIQUE constraint (no @Column(unique=true)), so duplicate provider refs and event ids insert fine
financial instants typed TIMESTAMP without time zone / mapped to LocalDateTime (issued_at, received_at, created_at)
handlePaymentSucceeded credits with no event-id dedup before the side effect, and the @PostMapping(/webhooks) controller has no guard either, so a redelivered webhook double-credits
chargeCustomer retries the POST to /charges with no Idempotency-Key header, a timed-out-but-successful first attempt is charged again on retry
creditAccount and debitAccount do a findById read-modify-write on the balance with no @Version, no row lock, and no atomic UPDATE
correctEntry mutates the posted entry amount in place (setAmount) instead of appending a reversing entry
postEntry records a single signed leg, no paired debit and credit, no shared transaction id, so debits and credits are never forced to balance
the account balance is a cached mutable column (Account.balance) incremented separately, never derived from summing the entries
entries and corrections carry no actor or reason, so a posted movement cannot say who moved the money or why
invoiceResponse serializes total as a bare JSON number (double via fromMinorUnits) and drops the currency from the payload
invoiceResponse returns decimal dollars while accountBalanceResponse returns integer cents, two endpoints disagree on the money unit with no canonical shape
no property-based tests, no jqwik @Property, so invariants like credited-once and sum-of-parts-equals-total are never generated against
every fixture amount is round (100, 50, 60, 40, 250.00, 10000), no fractional-cent or .xx5 tie case, so no rounding mode is ever distinguished
single happy currency USD, no JPY or BHD zero- or three-decimal case, no negative amount, no refund or reversal
